results.title: Semper Solaris is rated "Bad" with 1.4 / 5 on Trustpilot
results.content:
Feb 22, 2023

Image 48: Rated 1 out of 5 stars

## It started out great
It started out great! Great sales person, answered all our questions. Installation was good, technicians were very courteous and careful. That’s where it ended being good and went to terrible. Our system which includes solar panels and batteries has not worked correctly in 2 months, it was installed in December, it is now February. Customer Service is unresponsive. We are so disappointed and frustrated.

December 23, 2022

Unprompted review

Useful 3

Share

Image 49

Yuliya Korchnoy US•7 reviews

Oct 24, 2022

Image 50: Rated 1 out of 5 stars [...] 1. The individual who was sent to give me the quote, Cody, was late with no call or text indicating that he was running late. I had to call.

2. Once here, I learned he had not done gutters or the type of corners I was having trouble with.

3. He tried to upsell me with other services. Running through a list.

Tried to sell me solar, roof, air conditioning, house wind. I already had solar with a well known company that is very much still in existence. But he did not even recognize the name of Sunlux, assumed they were out of business. (Nope)

4. Never received the quote he promised, to be sent hours later.

Seems their focus is really solar or air conditioning. Not gutters. Why even quote me then? Why respond ? I said it was an extremely small project with photos. [...] Thus began the nightmare that we will call customer service from Semper Solaris. The never answered phone calls and always returned emails with generic responses that did not address my problem or how to fix it. Eventually we spent 2 weeks going back and forth verifying that the system was in fact not working and that my internet connection was not the problem (despite the fact that I verified that the system was not feeding the battery so it was obviously a larger problem). By middle of May after several emails with photos of my system with no lights showing (because it was broken) we scheduled a service tech to come out in the beginning of June. That service tech merely verified that the system was in fact broken and I needed a new inverter (that they would have to order from the

results.title: Semper Solaris Review (2026 Guide)
results.content:
“It started out great! Great sales person, answered all our questions. Installation was good, technicians were very courteous and careful. That’s where it ended being good and went to terrible. Our system which includes solar panels and batteries has not worked correctly in 2 months, it was installed in December, it is now February. Customer Service is unresponsive. We are so disappointed and frustrated.” –Carole D.

## Our Conclusion

If you live in California cities like San Diego and Sacramento, we recommend Semper Solaris for its extensive solar services and customer service, which are at the forefront of this veteran-owned business. Alternatively, there are a wide variety of well-reviewed solar companies in California.

### Other Solar Company Reviews [...] “The crew worked well. My wife or myself was there every day. They were always early and always cleaned up after themselves. They completed the work quickly and everything went very smoothly. We are up and running and so far so good! We are in the black with what we are selling back to the energy company.” –Jim B.

“Our crew was very professional. They let us know when they arrived and left and explained the work they were going to do each day. Their workmanship was good and it was clear that they were dedicated to doing a good job. They took personal interest in their work. We feel good about our decision to install solar.” –Wes C.

### Negative Reviews [...] ### Negative Reviews

“The sales and installation guys are great. But when one spends 20-30 thousand dollars you expect better customer service. They ignore your first several calls then when they do answer it’s like they are doing you a favor. If I had to do it all over again I would go with a different company.” –Kory F.

“Started solar project early February with semper solaris. As of now, 5 months later, I do not have a fully operation system and have to start payments…Wish I would of read some of the other reviews b/c I feel the same and picked another company.” –Trustpilot User

results.title: 3 Solar Companies You May Want To Avoid (According To Consumer Complaints)
results.content:
On TrustPilot, Semper Solaris has an overwhelmingly high number of 1-star reviews versus more positive ratings. Complaints include issues like difficulty obtaining service after installation and leaky roof problems that reportedly went unaddressed by Semper Solaris. Unlike other solar companies, Semper Solaris does not appear to be involved in as many lawsuits, but one class-action lawsuit stands out; A group of consumers sued Semper Solaris for recording their phone calls without permission or warning.

With so many myths about solar panels and various guidelines, like the 33% rule for solar panel installation, it's important that you choose a trustworthy company to handle your solar project. According to various consumer reviews and complaints, Semper Solaris might not be one of those. [...] ## Semper Solaris

Couple standing next to house with solar panels

Another solar company you might want to avoid, if past consumer complaints are any indication, is Semper Solaris. Semper Solaris has overall poor reviews, and it's also facing legal action in court. Semper Solaris does have an A+ BBB rating, and it's accredited. However, a few hundred complaints indicate that working with Semper Solaris as a consumer getting solar installed isn't always a walk in the park. [...] In case you're one of those homeowners who gets a knock on your door from a solar salesperson, or before buying a house with solar, it may help to know which companies you might want to avoid. Not every solar company operates the same way, and there may be some fine print to sift through with any one of them. Yet there are some that are considered more reliable than others, based on consumer reviews.

As a homeowner who's been on the receiving end of some door knocks that ended in frustration, I wasn't surprised to find out that other consumers have similar complaints after dealing with a few specific solar companies. Here are three that may be best avoided, if the consumer complaints, BBB ratings, and lawsuits are anything to go off of.

## Sunrun

results.title: Semper Solaris solar reviews, complaints, address & solar panels cost
results.content:
Brad G | San Clemente, CA | 12/02/2025

not recommended... look for a different company

I have had nothing but service issue after service issue. tech support is a nightmare and not proactive. I have to follow up on everything and even after paying $700 for the service the system didn't get fixed and is actually performing worse. I have even reached out to their senior leadership directly... they hand off to support, no follow up or follow through. Just a disappointing experience overall with this company. I think anyone looking to use them is rolling the dice and I would definitely recommend going with another provider.

clh562 | Cerritos, CA | 11/21/2025

RUN! They tried to close the project with a HOLE IN THE ROOF. [...] Felix | Spring Valley, CA | 10/09/2025

Bad micro inverter (panel)

We had our solar and roof installed July of 2022. Clean, fast and courteous. 1 panel went out May 25 2024 and here we are Aug 02 20203 and we still have 1 panel not working. Bad customer service to get some one out to replace or repair the panel. Email after email, no date of when we can get this resolved. Been paying for all panels monthly as usual. Will we not billed the months we have been without that panel, I think not. I should have read reviews or researched this better.

Nestico | Los Angeles, CA | 10/09/2025

So Happy [...] 2 years and still having issues, no direct contact number

I initiated a new roof and solar project under net metering 2 with Semper in 2023. It took over a year and a half to get the project done, with several issues along the way, including a bait an switch on the "light weight" ie. less durable product. Every time they have to address a concern, the break numerous tiles (over 200 last time). After the first major rain, we have a leak in my girls bedroom coming from a light. They refuse to supply their general liability information (policy number and provider) so I can submit a claim through insurance instead (which is illegal). 3 weeks later, still a tarp on the roof with no scheduled time to fix it permanently. Awful, Awful, Awful.

Brad G | San Clemente, CA | 12/02/2025

results.title: Semper Solaris Review: Costs, Quality, Services & More (2026)
results.content:
– Solomon F. via BBB

### Negative Semper Solaris Reviews

“My project for solar batteries is 2 years in the making and still no work has been started. The salespeople are very good at making you believe they have superior service but don’t be fooled. Their communication is poor at best [and] nonexistent frequently. My rebate from PG&E has had to be extended because this is taking so long and the extension is expiring again.”

– Quo E. via Yelp [...] ## Semper Solaris Reviews

Customer reviews are one of the best ways to truly judge a solar installer’s quality, and Semper has received hundreds of reviews from customers over the years. While the majority of these are positive and laud Semper’s knowledge and follow-up, there are plenty of negative reviews that cite slow or disappointing service/support. Let’s take a look at a few examples.

### Positive Semper Solaris Reviews [...] ### Positive Semper Solaris Reviews

“Excellent! My solar ground-mount looks great and although it took awhile to be completed because the County of SD is very slow we are happy. The crew was gentle when working around my wife’s plants and garden which we very much appreciated. We worked with Rhami during the sales process and he was informative and always available to us which made us feel in good hands.”

– Peter D. via Trustpilot

“Excellent service. The technicians who installed the panels as well as the technicians who installed the battery back up were all very professional, friendly, and did a wonderful job cleaning up after their work. They work long hard days until the whole installation in two days. They were exceedingly professional and a joy to have worked for us.”
